claim_boundary: The route proves only the declared three-value content-admission boundary, UI states, tasks, paths, and current observed/implementation evidence; it does not certify unvisited behavior, authorization roles, future AI behavior, or visual design quality.

closure_rules.scope: current target revision and declared route only

forbidden_shortcuts.summary: Do not create a SkillGuard-owned alternate execution route.
Do not convert configured booleans, generated JSON, or progress into native pass evidence.
Do not skip required stages, checks, receipts, or blockers.

integration_claim_boundary: SkillGuard gates the target-native FlowGuard route; it does not execute a parallel controller or replace native model/test evidence.

native_check_bindings.evidence_source: current model runner receipt and final report

native_check_inventory.summary: Run UI content-admission, flow, visible-surface, on-demand operability, and implementation evidence model checks.

native_route_bindings.source: FlowGuard canonical route registry and target contract source

not_parallel_route_proof.evidence_source: Canonical route registry, contract source, and may_define_* false gates
not_parallel_route_proof.summary: SkillGuard only validates the FlowGuard-owned route and cannot define an alternate successful execution path.

phases.summary: Declare greenfield, source_based, or mixed mode; product/user scope; UI candidate boundary; source authority; and requested UI claim, without inventing audience roles.

Inventory observed visible items without granting permission; inventory candidate displayed/status/helper/metadata/detail content, required capabilities/outputs, tasks, controls, displays, states, events, source mappings, and blindspots.

Classify candidate content with the exact three values and typed resolvable needs; narrow control-label exemptions; bind all-target disclosure state, task-owned affordance, item feedback, observed inventory, and structured per-content evidence into downstream contracts.

Run the UI Flow model and reject unclassified/internal mappings, overbroad control-label exemptions, direct observed/display bypass, invalid all-target on-demand state/accessibility, missing item feedback, unowned enabled actions, stale/opaque/cross-content evidence, or design-only runnable claims.
Return the current content-visibility plan, UI evidence, and gaps; hand semantic transition cells and large evidence matrices to MTA/TestMesh.

quality_floors.summary: Prompt prose, progress, or generated records cannot replace native evidence.
Missing, stale, skipped, scoped, progress-only, or pass-with-gaps evidence cannot support broad closure.

required_evidence.source: FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact

route_inventory.summary: Admit candidate UI content, model UI behavior, and prove observed surface, capability/task coverage, journeys, on-demand operability, implementation evidence, geometry, and transition scope.

do_not_use_when: The work is visual styling only, non-UI, code-structure planning, audience/permission taxonomy design, or prose/observed visibility/label/API evidence is being substituted for content admission or UI behavior proof.
routes.summary: Admit candidate UI content, model UI behavior, and prove observed surface, capability/task coverage, journeys, on-demand operability, implementation evidence, geometry, and transition scope.

runtime_lock_policy.applies_when: The flowguard-ui-flow-structure skill is selected or delegated.
runtime_lock_policy.summary: The target-local contract, route, stage order, checks, evidence, and coverage matrix gate closure.

source_requirements.summary: Accept typed admission, a complete UI state model, task/control coverage, and current evidence; block leaks, unsafe disclosure, blindspots, or stale proof.
Route ui_flow_structure; role public_owner; native owner ui_flow_structure. This standalone FlowGuard satellite skill owns content admission and UI evidence, not styling, code structure, or.
Use for displayed/status/helper/metadata content, optional details, controls, visible surface, tasks, actions, runs, or layout evidence.
The target section 'Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Design evidence cannot prove runnable UI; source, recovery, freshness, and harvest gaps stay explicit.
Every reachable enabled action needs control -> event -> owner -> function -> UI update -> evidence, a pure-UI disposition, or a blindspot.
The target section 'Hard Gates' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Return the visibility plan, UI state diagram, task/control coverage, blindspots, evidence, failures, blockers, skipped_checks, residual_risk, claim_boundary, and typed_next_actions.
Unclassified/internal content cannot render; do not add audience/role/persona categories.
user_on_demand stays hidden across display/text/visible/observed mappings. Reveal/return controls are visible, enabled, labeled, task-owned, and tied to content feedback; hover uses a disti.
Verify the real FlowGuard check engine and AGENTS.md managed project record; never create a fake mini-framework.
The target skill description must be represented in the contract: Use for UI content admission, observed surface, behavior, structure/text, operability, evidence, or transitions.
The target skill entrypoint and hard gates must be represented by the runtime contract.
description: Use for UI content admission, observed surface, behavior, structure/text, operability, evidence, or transitions.
Edit .skillguard/contract-source.json, regenerate derived contracts, and never treat SkillGuard as click/screenshot/DOM/human evidence.
Declared hard gates and required workflow steps must be represented by checks and closure blockers.
Do not style first, invent roles, or treat observed visibility, labels, APIs, or prose as permission/proof; return non-UI work to model-first-function-flow.
The target section 'Do Not Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'Output Requirements' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Admit content, model UI event x UI state behavior, and prove runnable evidence.
Bind claims to the plan, observed inventory, structured content rows, screenshot/DOM/event/result evidence, click chains, blindspots, and tests.
Classify candidates only as user_visible, user_on_demand, or internal; require typed/resolvable task/state/recovery/safety needs. Exempt only the exact normal label of a registered, in-scop.
Declare greenfield, source_based, or mixed; observation never grants display permission.
Read references/ui_flow_structure_protocol.md; it routes observed surface, interaction, journey/structure/text, human, implementation, and geometry protocols.
The target section 'Required Workflow' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.

Two conceptual groups use exactly user_visible, user_on_demand, or internal for every in-scope state-exposing candidate; typed needs must resolve, unclassified/internal content cannot render, and only exact labels for registered in-scope task-owned controls without extra state or metadata need no duplicate row.
Model/design evidence cannot prove implemented/runnable completion; source alignment, recovery, human, and current evidence-kind gates remain explicit.
Every reachable enabled action has a modeled event and current functional chain/click evidence, pure-UI disposition, or owned blindspot.
Content admission keeps exactly three visibility values in two conceptual groups and does not add audience, role, persona, expert-mode, or authorization categories.
Existing/runnable UI claims require complete observed-surface and user-visible capability/output accounting, while observation itself grants no display permission.

User_on_demand stays hidden across display/text/visible/observed mappings until visible/enabled task-owned reveal with item-bound feedback, has an operable return path, and uses a distinct keyboard/focus event for hover.

Runnable claims bind the current plan and observed inventory to structured per-content default/reveal/revealed/return/internal-absence evidence; positive rows resolve to the exact content and state, and a boolean, opaque, or unrelated observation is insufficient.
